judgment.summary: A highly profitable, cash-generative business with strong returns on equity that is currently struggling with significant earnings contraction.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 2.70 beat the 2.68 estimate by 0.9%, with the next earnings date scheduled for 2026-07-15.
positives: Profit margin 21.8% — indicates strong bottom-line efficiency.
Return on equity 26.4% — shows high efficiency in generating profits from equity.
Forward P/E 18.13 vs trailing 26.67 — suggests a more attractive valuation on a forward basis.
Free cash flow (TTM) $12.51B — provides substantial cash generation.
negatives: Earnings growth -52.9% — reflects a major year-over-year decline in profitability.
PEG ratio 2.91 — suggests the valuation is high relative to growth.
Quick ratio 0.69 — indicates tight immediate liquidity.
Quarterly earnings growth -52.4% — shows a sharp recent drop in earnings.

news.paragraph: Johnson & Johnson is seeing positive momentum following an upgrade from Leerink Partners, which raised its price target to $265 and moved the rating to Outperform. Market sentiment is also being bolstered by growth expectations surrounding the company's blockbuster drug prospects. Options traders should weigh this recent analyst optimism and drug development potential when assessing the stock's direction.
headlines: JNJ gets Wall Street boost as blockbuster drug bets ignite growth hopes
